commit.message:
Hearth: Queue appears empty (Forge-v5z) (#39)

* fix: Hearth queue reads from daemon's cached poll data instead of polling independently

The Hearth TUI was running its own `bd ready --json` polls every 2 seconds,
independent of the daemon. This caused the queue to appear empty when:
- The bd ready call failed silently (connection drops, timeouts)
- Race conditions between daemon claiming beads and Hearth polling

Fix: The daemon now writes poll results to a queue_cache table in SQLite
(shared state DB). The Hearth reads from this cache instead of running
its own bd ready subprocess. This follows the same pattern used for
workers and events.

Bead: Forge-v5z

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-v5z

- Skip queue cache replacement when all anvil polls fail to avoid
  wiping the UI on transient errors
- Return nil from FetchQueue on DB read error to preserve previous
  queue state in Hearth TUI
- Use prepared statement in ReplaceQueueCache for better performance
- Add TestDB_QueueCache covering replacement semantics and ordering

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-v5z

- Add anvil to ORDER BY in QueueCache() for deterministic sort when
  the same bead_id appears in multiple anvils
- Skip ReplaceQueueCache when beads is empty and at least one anvil
  poll failed, preventing partial failures from wiping the cache
- Add test case for duplicate bead IDs across anvils verifying
  deterministic tie-break ordering

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-v5z

- Replace os.Stderr write in Hearth FetchQueue with returning an
  UpdateQueueMsg so errors don't corrupt the TUI in alt-screen mode
- Change ReplaceQueueCache to ReplaceQueueCacheForAnvils: only deletes
  and re-inserts rows for successfully polled anvils, preserving
  last-known cache for failed anvils
- Update daemon poll loop to collect succeeded anvil names and call
  the per-anvil cache update, so partial poll failures don't wipe
  the queue for unrelated anvils
- Add test for per-anvil retention behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-v5z

- Fix misleading test comment to match what the test actually exercises
- Filter items by allowed anvil set in ReplaceQueueCacheForAnvils to
  prevent inserting items for anvils not in the replacement set

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-v5z

- Update hearth command help text to state daemon must be running
- Preserve previous queue on QueueCache error instead of clearing it
- Surface queue read errors in the events panel for operator visibility

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-v5z

Prepend synthetic error events instead of appending so they appear
as the newest event in the newest-first event list, and pin scroll
to top in follow mode.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: collect() before hour mutate to avoid DuckDB tz translation error

DuckDB lazy tables can't translate as.POSIXct(tz="UTC") to SQL.
Move collect() before the hour floor mutate in dv_temporal_coverage
and dv_temporal_gaps targets. Fixes CI run 22765120045.

Closes #45

commit.message:
fix(perf): Analysis-$lookup aus Pipeline entfernt + Collection-Name fix

1. Analysis-$lookup mit $expr/$toString aus Aggregation-Pipeline entfernt —
   konnte keine Indexes nutzen und scannte die Collection bei jedem Vertrag.
   Ersetzt durch 2 indexed Batch-Queries (per _id + Fallback per Name),
   gleiche bewährte Logik wie enrichContractWithAnalysis.

2. Collection-Name-Bug gefixt: $lookup referenzierte "analysis" (singular),
   aber Daten liegen in "analyses" (plural). Auch Index-Erstellung in
   server.js korrigiert.

3. Events + Envelopes $lookups bleiben in der Pipeline (indexed, schnell).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Sprint 4-6: Complete assessment system overhaul

Sprint 4 — User Feedback Fixes:
- MD exports now include results summary (band description, section
  breakdown with percentages and strength indicators) before Q&A
- Removed all "AI analysis" references, replaced with "advanced
  analysis and recommendation"
- Strategy Selector: removed email screen, stepper starts immediately
- Geography question updated to Gulf/Levant/North Africa/MENA Broadly/Global
- Replaced all Slack references with Telegram
- Renamed "EO MicroSaaS" to "Entrepreneurs Oasis MENA" everywhere
  (including H1 headings with <span> tags)
- Fixed Dashboard: localStorage fallback, Load Local Data button,
  demand matrix redesign

Sprint 5 — Hub + Flow:
- Dashboard hub redesign with completion state badges (✅ DONE + score)
- Score persistence: all 5 scorecards save to eo_completion_status
- Generate Project Brief button (visible when all 5 complete)
- Flow enforcement with prerequisite checks (ICP→MAS→Strategy→GTM→PF)

Sprint 6 — Auth + Docs + QA:
- Consistent email auth via eo_user_email localStorage key
- Auto-skip email screen on subsequent scorecards
- Dashboard auto-fills lookup email
- Added README.md with full system documentation
- Added Project Files scorecard, config, and SKILL.md
- Full E2E browser testing: visual, mobile, functional all passing

commit.message:
fix: bake VITE_API_URL into frontend at creation + trigger redeploy

The frontend static site was created with empty envVars, so Render's
auto-deploy built without VITE_API_URL. The env var was set after
creation but the build was already running.

- Pass VITE_API_URL at createRenderStaticSite time (backendUrl is
  already resolved before frontend creation)
- Add triggerManualDeploy() helper
- After confirming env vars are saved, wait 3s then trigger a manual
  redeploy to guarantee the frontend builds with correct VITE_API_URL

and host config fix from the entire debugging session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Schematic pre-worker to decompose large or ambiguous beads before Smith starts (Forge-70e) (#43)

* feat: add Schematic pre-worker to decompose large or ambiguous beads before Smith starts

Add a new 'Schematic' pre-worker that runs before Smith in the pipeline
(fitting the forge metaphor: the schematic is drawn before the smith starts
forging). The Schematic:

- Spawns a lightweight AI session to analyse bead scope and complexity
- Can produce a focused implementation plan injected into Smith's prompt
- Can decompose large beads into sub-beads via bd, blocking the parent
- Can flag beads needing human clarification (releases back to open)
- Is gated by word-count heuristic (default 100) or 'decompose' tag
- Is globally opt-in via settings.schematic_enabled in forge.yaml
- Can be disabled per-anvil via schematic_enabled: false

New files:
- internal/schematic/schematic.go — core logic, prompt, verdict parsing
- internal/schematic/schematic_test.go — unit tests for ShouldRun, parsing

Modified:
- internal/config/config.go — SchematicEnabled, SchematicWordThreshold settings
- internal/pipeline/pipeline.go — Schematic integration before Smith loop
- internal/pipeline/pipeline_test.go — 6 new tests covering all Schematic paths
- internal/prompt/prompt.go — SchematicPlan field + template section
- internal/state/db.go — schematic_started/done/skipped event types
- internal/daemon/daemon.go — wire Schematic config to pipeline params

Bead: Forge-70e

- Remove duplicated step numbering in pipeline.go comments

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Fix schematic import alphabetical ordering in daemon.go

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-70e

- Fix misleading comment on SchematicWordThreshold default

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-70e

- Only set NeedsHuman=true when bead release succeeds in Clarify path
- Add ExtraFlags field to schematic.Config and wire claude_flags in daemon
- Replace fmt.Sprintf with strings.Builder in buildPrompt to avoid format

- Add TestSchematic_Clarify_NeedsHumanFalse_WhenReleaseFails test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-70e

- Use blocks: dep instead of discovered-from: so sub-beads actually
  block the parent in bd ready (Comment 1)
- Update pipeline package doc to mention Schematic pre-worker step

commit.message:
refactor: serve APK via built-in HTTP server instead of volume mounts

The Go HTTP server now serves the apk/ directory at /apk/.
Appium containers run with --network=host so they can fetch the APK
from http://localhost:8080/apk/filename.apk without any volume mounts
or internet access.

- web: add ServeAPKDir() registering /apk/ file handler
- manager: remove bind-mount logic, pass APIDEMOS_APK_URL instead
- main: construct APK serve URL and register it with web server
- Config: APKPath → APKServeURL

commit.message:
fix(ci): move all code signing settings to pbxproj, not command line

CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY="Apple Distribution" on the xcodebuild command line
also applies to Firebase SPM dependency targets, causing "conflicting
provisioning settings" errors. Move CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY into the pbxproj
injection step alongside CODE_SIGN_STYLE, DEVELOPMENT_TEAM, and
PROVISIONING_PROFILE so they only affect the App target.

commit.message:
fix: correct tetrahedron face winding to CCW (outward-facing)

All four tetrahedron faces had clockwise winding when viewed from
outside, causing backface culling to hide exterior faces and show
only interior ones. Reversed each triangle's vertex order so the
cross-product normals point away from the centroid.

- Add output contract at top defining response format
- Deduplicate silent transfer rules (was repeated 6x, now defined once in section 4)
- Deduplicate tool enforcement rules (was repeated 3x, now consolidated in section 3)
- Replace XML tags with clean markdown sections
- Establish real priority hierarchy (MUST/NEVER instead of everything being CRITICAL)
- Remove 50+ emoji urgency markers, use bold text instead
- Separate personality (section 1) from per-response controls (section 2)

- Reduce from 734 to ~470 lines (~35% reduction) with zero business logic loss

commit.message:
feat: add streaming read path to avoid full result materialisation

Adds query_batched() in helpers and read_batched() on IsauraReader.
DuckDB now streams results via fetch_record_batch() so peak RAM is
bounded to one batch at a time instead of the full matched result set.
read() and all existing callers are untouched.

commit.message:
Add one-liner bootstrap install script

curl -sSL .../bootstrap.sh | sudo bash handles everything: installs
git if needed, clones the repo, fixes ownership, and runs the full
install script. Eliminates the chicken-and-egg problem of needing git
to clone the repo that contains the install script.

commit.message:
Fix exchange rate cron disappearing after deactivation cycles

The refresh cron was only scheduled in register_activation_hook. If WordPress
cleared it (deactivation, options reset, cron cleanup), rates went stale with
no recovery path. The init hook now re-schedules automatically when the event
is missing. Bump to 1.1.2.

commit.message:
fix: use multi-mesh buffer cache to prevent destroyed-buffer panic

LoadGeometry now caches all mesh GPU buffers in a map instead of
destroying the previous mesh's buffers on each call. This prevents
the "Buffer has been destroyed" panic during multi-mesh render passes
where all buffers must remain alive until queue submission.

Shutdown iterates the cache to release all entries.
